assumptions: Assume the intragroup dividend receivable/payable is a foreign-currency monetary item between group entities with different functional currencies, so the related exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.
Assume the entity has an eligible hedging instrument and formal hedge designation and documentation that satisfy IFRS 9 paragraph 6.4.1.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup foreign-currency receivable can be considered for a fair value hedge of its foreign exchange risk when the intragroup monetary item exception applies.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also permits cash flow hedging of a particular risk on a recognised asset or liability, making the receivable's foreign-currency cash flow variability a distinct hedge accounting model to consider.
